heading: Tyagarah vs Tyalgum Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Tyagarah, New South Wales and Tyalgum Creek, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Tyagarah has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Tyalgum Creek (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Tyagarah or Tyalgum Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Tyagarah has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Tyalgum Creek scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Tyagarah scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Tyalgum Creek scores 45/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Tyagarah or Tyalgum Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Tyagarah has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
